changes_made: Compressed skill from 236 lines to 101 lines (57% reduction)
Added IMPORTANT WORKFLOW section with CWD and execution guidance
Added complete runnable File Checksums pattern with os.path.basename
Added complete runnable Word Counting pattern with Counter merge
Explicit instruction: use f.read().split() — no regex, no .lower(), no .strip()
Explicit instruction: do NOT use sort_keys=True for word count output
Explicit instruction: use os.path.basename() for output dict keys
Removed sections: Loop-to-Map, Output Ordering, Shared State, Resource Management, Error Handling, Performance tuning, ProcessPoolExecutor details

notes.parallel-checksum: Consistently scores 93/100 across all 5 iterations. The all-files-present criterion scores 0.5 despite correct output — appears to be a test evaluation framework issue (all checksums verified correct, all 30 entries present, all filenames match pattern).
notes.parallel-wordcount: Scores vary between 100 (best), 93 (typical), and 10 (worst). Score of 10 occurs when the runtime fails to produce output (CWD/execution issue, not skill content). Score of 93 vs 100 is due to tied word counts at 124 (three words tied, only two fit in top 10) causing unstable sort results in the test.
